---
title: "Ammar Eltigani"
subtitle: "PhD Student in Mathematics"
---

::: {.hero}

::: {.hero-image}
![](images/profile.png){fig-alt="Profile image placeholder" width="240"}
:::

::: {.hero-text}

## Ammar Eltigani

I am a first-year PhD student in mathematics at the University of Michigan. My research interests include algebraic geometry, number theory, and related areas.

I am interested in questions at the interface of geometry, arithmetic, and representation theory. More broadly, I enjoy mathematical exposition, teaching, and learning how different parts of mathematics talk to each other.


## Research Interests

- Algebraic geometry
- Number theory
- Arithmetic geometry
- Representation theory
- Moduli spaces
- Homological and cohomological methods

## Recent Updates

- **May 2026:** Gave a talk in the Graduate Algebraic Geometry Seminar.
- **March 2026:** Posted expository notes on spectral sequences.
- **January 2026:** Attended the Joint Mathematics Meetings.
- **December 2025:** Updated CV and research statement.


## Teaching Experience

### University of Michigan

#### Instructor of Record (GSI)

- **Math XXX: Course Name**, Summer 2026.

## Mentoring and Outreach

- Mentored undergraduate students in mathematics.
- Assisted with graduate student seminars and reading groups.

## CV

[Download PDF CV](files/cv.pdf){.button .button-primary}

Last updated: May 2026.

## Contact

Email: `altigani@umich.edu`  
Office: East Hall, University of Michigan  
GitHub: [ammareltigani](https://github.com/ammareltigani)  
Google Scholar: [Profile link](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=3uzn-m0AAAAJ)
